concept.conceptName: Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay
Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Nephelometry

en.name: Fibrinogen antigen
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Fibrinogen antigen is the **immunological measurement of the fibrinogen protein concentration** in plasma — what this concept set measures. It quantifies how much fibrinogen protein is present, irrespective of whether the molecule is functionally clottable by thrombin [1]. The result is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, produced on platelet poor plasma (PPP) by immunoassay [2]. Results are reported as a mass concentration in g/L or mg/dL [3]. Clinically, fibrinogen antigen aids in the diagnosis of fibrinogen deficiencies (acquired or congenital) and is used for monitoring the severity and treatment of disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) and fibrinolysis [2]. **Note: "fibrinogen" vs "fibrinogen antigen".** This concept set covers **fibrinogen antigen** only — the immunological mass of the fibrinogen protein. The functionally clottable fibrinogen, measured by clot-based or viscoelastic assays, is captured by a separate concept set, "Fibrinogen in blood". The two values can diverge in dysfibrinogenaemia, where antigen is normal but the clot-based result is reduced — they are different study variables and must not be merged. ## Included Concepts The set contains two LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, both measuring fibrinogen antigenic concentration in platelet poor plasma, differing only by the assay method: - *3016628 / 3256-5 — Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay*: generic immunoassay code. Most widely used. - *3037950 / 42772-4 — Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Nephelometry*: same quantity, with the nephelometry method explicit. **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mg/dL`** for both codes. Source systems may also report in `g/L`; conversion `1 g/L = 100 mg/dL`. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The following LOINC concepts that exist for fibrinogen are deliberately not part of this set (they belong to other concept sets): - **Fibrinogen by clot-based or viscoelastic assay**: captured by "Fibrinogen in blood". - **Fibrinogen Ag/Fibrinogen [Mass Ratio]** (`3026131 / 31158-9`): a derived ratio used to detect dysfibrinogenaemia, computed downstream from the activity and antigen values — not a primary measurement. - **Fibrinogen [Presence]** / **Fibrinogen [Interpretation]**: qualitative concepts. - **Cryofibrinogen, Fibrinogen fragments, Fibrin+Fibrinogen fragments**: different measurands. - **Tissue and Smears specimens**: out of scope. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source explicitly reports "fibrinogen antigen", "fibrinogen Ag": `3016628 / 3256-5 — Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay`. - **Source explicitly states nephelometry method**: map to `3037950 / 42772-4 — Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Nephelometry`. - **Source reports a single "fibrinogen" without specifying activity vs antigen**: by default, map to the "Fibrinogen in blood" concept set — the clot-based assay is the routine clinical measurement; antigen is a specialty test. Only map to this antigen concept set when the source unambiguously labels the result as "Ag" / "antigen". - **Source unit is mg/dL**: most LOINC codes for fibrinogen Ag have `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS = mg/dL`, but European labs typically report in `g/L`. Conversion: `1 g/L = 100 mg/dL`. - **Do not confuse with**: fibrinogen by clot-based assay (separate concept set), fibrinogen fragments (degradation products), D-dimer, cryofibrinogen. ## References [1] Mosesson MW. Fibrinogen and fibrin structure and functions. *J Thromb Haemost*. 2005;3(8):1894-904. Available: https://doi.org/10.1111/j.1538-7836.2005.01365.x [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3256-5 — Fibrinogen Ag [Mass/volume] in Platelet poor plasma by Immunoassay. Available: https://loinc.org/3256-5/ [3] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
